provenance.screenshots: private native-screenshot corpus (not in this distribution) superseded by the NATIVE ORACLE CAPTURES tools/harness/results/truemenu-native/*.json (menu_oracle.lua, paused-capture protocol, 2026-07-08).
notes: v2 REWRITE (tmcontent-executor 2026-07-08): every leaf-ORDER expectation now comes from the actual button[] row order of a native oracle capture, NOT the montages. DF's native leaf order is NEVER alphabetical (alpha_order=0 on every captured row); the v1 fixtures encoded an alpha misreading (B45).

Capture provenance per category: weapons iron=01, armor glowing-metal=07 (only armor LEAF capture; iron armor order is derived from the same family rule but marked set-only since no iron armor leaf capture exists), furniture gold=08 (order is metal-independent -> encoded for iron), metal clothing adamantine=27, OTHER iron=29 / silver=21, instruments INSTRUMENT_PIECE=28, root=16.
The gate runs the generator against the SAME live fort these captures came from (active forge id 96, fort civ ENT305), so civ-rolled clothing + world-generated instrument names match byte-for-byte.
Metal-list panels inside the montages were a reused weapons crop; only the weapons + other-objects metal lists are trustworthy metal-list captures (silver has no ITEMS_ARMOR).
Trap leaf ORDER is NOT-VERIFIED (no trap LEAF capture) -> set-equality only.

_comment_trap_metals: B52: native TRAP metal list (capture 20) == native WEAPONS metal list (17 rows, ITEMS_WEAPON only), NOT the ITEMS_HARD 35. These HARD-only metals must NOT appear in the trap metal list.

instrument_piece_note: capture 28: 9 leaves, ALL fort-civ (MAKE_ENT305) CustomReaction, leaf-ONLY category (no metal layer), RAWS order. World-generated names are worldgen-specific + carry CP437-accented bytes (azkob/shosel), so the gate asserts count + kind + leaf-only shape structurally and lists only the ASCII names as a must-contain subset; the exact accented names are diffed live via --served/--oracle against THIS fort's capture 28 (both sides same encoding).

smelter_reference_capture30.note: FLAT-SHOP follow-up (NOT implemented by tmcontent-executor -- getJobs-derived flat_root has no native label/order model). Encoded for the next agent. Native Smelter add-task root, capture 30.
smelter_reference_capture30.native_order_rule: melt row FIRST, then ores in inorganic raws-index order, then reactions in raws order -- never alphabetical (B45 class).

label_fidelity.ore_suffix: native 'Smelt hematite ore' (served drops ' ore' -> 'smelt hematite')
label_fidelity.melt_object: native 'Melt a metal object' (served 'Melt metal object')
label_fidelity.reaction_case: native capitalizes reaction names via text(); served serves lowercase raws names

smelter_reference_capture30.leaf_set: 40/40 clean -- content generation is correct; only label composition + order diverge.

craftsdwarf_reference_capture31.note: FLAT-SHOP SHAPE follow-up (NOT implemented). Native Craftsdwarf add-task root, capture 31, is NOT flat: 51 direct leaves + 4 material-selector submenu rows ('rock/wood/bone/shell (opens menu)') + 2 custom-category rows (Make instrument piece / Make instrument, natively PRESENT here -- independently confirms B40: the civ's instrument reactions attach to Craftsdwarf).
craftsdwarf_reference_capture31.shape_class: cat/leaf-and-material-submenu mixed at ROOT -- the shop-shape must be derived from DF's interface_category_building/job structures, not per-shop hardcoded. Served treats Craftsdwarfs as one flat 346-row list.

flatshop_notes: flatshop-executor 2026-07-08: native captures 30 (Smelter root, 40 rows), 31 (Craftsdwarf root, 57 rows -- 4 material selectors + 51 direct leaves + 2 instrument custom-categories), 32 (Craftsdwarf rock submenu, 19 material-pinned leaves), 33 (Kennels = v50 Vermin Catcher's Shop, 2 rows). Root labels/kinds are ASCII + deterministic (instrument LEAF names are worldgen and live one level below the root, so the root assertion is exact). Selector/custom-category rows are stored bare in the model (client appends ' (opens menu)'). wood/bone/shell submenu CONTENTS are NOT captured -> not gated.

montage_iron.provenance: wp3-executor 2026-07-08: montage corpus 'Menu Oracle Screenshots/flow images metalsmithing workshop iron/' (9 forge shots). A SECOND, INDEPENDENT oracle -- a DIFFERENT world/civ than the ENT305 native captures: its forge root has 9 categories ('Make instrument' is NON-empty here), different divine metals ('cosmic stardust'/'ruddy metal'/... vs ENT305's), and 'toga' where ENT305 rolls 'dress'. TWO montage caveats, both load-bearing: (1) leaves are shown ALPHABETIZED (search-sort toggle ON: Forge-verb block A-Z, then Make-verb block A-Z) -- so only leaf SETS are portable across the two oracles, NEVER order (the button[] captures give raws order, B45); (2) the metal panels under Armor/Siege/Trap/Metal-clothing are a REUSED weapons (ITEMS_WEAPON) crop -- silver appears under Armor (ITEMS_ARMOR excludes it), and the Metal-clothing shot highlights 'iron' yet lists adamantine leaves -- so montage METAL lists are NOT asserted. Only the civ/world-INDEPENDENT cells are gated: root category order (as an ordered subsequence -- ENT305's 8 vs this world's 9) and the weapons/armor/furniture/siege/trap iron leaf SETS. Metal-clothing + instrument leaves are civ/world-specific -> NOT asserted. The generated model's SETS match these montages exactly (differential agreement, VERIFIED offline).
